project.title: KiP analysis of ER+ Breast Cancer
project.projectDescription: Endocrine therapies (ET) combined with CDK4/6 inhibition (CDK4/6i) is standard treatment for estrogen receptor-α-positive (ER+) breast cancer, however lethal drug resistance is common.  Proteogenomic analyses of 22 ER+ breast cancer patient-derived xenografts (PDXs) demonstrated that PKMYT1, a WEE1 homolog, is highly estradiol (E2) regulated in E2-dependent PDXs and constitutively expressed when growth is E2 independent. In clinical samples, high PKMYT1 mRNA is a resistance biomarker for both ET and CDK4/6 inhibition. The PKMYT1 inhibitor lunresertib(RP-6306) and gemcitabine selectively and synergistically reduced the viability of ET and palbociclib-resistant ER+ breast cancer cells without functional p53 in vitro; the combination increased DNA damage and apoptosis. In palbociclib-resistant, TP53 mutant PDX organoids and xenografts, RP-6306 with low-dose gemcitabine induced greater tumor volume reduction compared to treatment with either single agent. These results demonstrate the clinical potential of RP-6306 in combination with gemcitabine for ET and CDK4/6i resistant TP53 mutant ER+ breast cancer.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: MCF7 and T47D cells (parental and Palbo-R) are maintained in RPMI 1640 media with 10% FBS (Sigma F0926), 1% penicillin-streptomycin and Plasmocin® Prophylactic (5 μg/ml).  MCF7 EDR and MCF7 EDR Palbo-R cells are maintained in phenol red-free RPMI 1640 with 10% charcoal-stripped FBS (Sigma F6765), 1% penicillin-streptomycin and Plasmocin® Prophylactic (5 μg/ml). The Palbo-R cell lines were also maintained with 1μM palbociclib added. A detergent based native protein lysate (50 mM HEPES (pH 7.5) 0.5% Triton X-100, 150mM NaCl) was used to lyse cells. 5KiPv2 kinobeads were washed in a high salt buffer and then equilibrated with lysis buffer. Lysates were incubated with kinobeads for 1 hour at 4 oC with rotation. Kinobeads were then washed with a high salt detergent buffer (50 mM HEPES (pH 7.5), 600 mM NaCl, 1 mM EDTA, 1 mM EGTA with 0.5% Triton X-100) and subsequently with lower salt and detergent until the final two washes with pure water. On-bead digestion was performed by adding 30 μL of 100 mM NH4HCO3 and heating at 65oC for 10 min followed by the addition of 250ng of Trypsin and an overnight incubation at 37 oC. A detergent removal kit (HiPPR Detergent Removal Kit (Thermo Scientific)) was used before samples were dried in a speed-vac. 5KiPv2 kinobeads, one quarter or one sixth of peptides were loaded onto a EvoSep with a 8 cm column (EV 1109) and a 30 μm steel emitter. A 44minute gradient was used and custom SureQuant acquision scheme was used on an Exploris 480 (Thermo). 106 stable isotope labeled peptides were added at 10 fmol on column.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw files were analyzed using Skyline and a SureQuant generated analysis scheme. All peaks were checked manually to ensure accurate quantification. Ratios to heavy peptide standards were computed and reported. 3 fragment ions were required for quantification and interference ions were manually removed from analysis.

references.referenceLine: Chen A, Kim BJ, Mitra A, Vollert CT, Lei JT, Fandino D, Anurag M, Holt MV, Gou X, Pilcher JB, Goetz MP, Northfelt DW, Hilsenbeck SG, Marshall CG, Hyer ML, Papp R, Yin SY, De Angelis C, Schiff R, Fuqua SAW, Ma CX, Foulds CE, Ellis MJ. PKMYT1 Is a Marker of Treatment Response and a Therapeutic Target for CDK4/6 Inhibitor-Resistance in ER+ Breast Cancer. Mol Cancer Ther. 2024 23(10):1494-1510
